title: CosMx Protein slide preparation
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"c3gbg","text":"This protocol details the CosMx protein slide preparation.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Samples were processed according to the guidelines provided in the CosMx SMI slide preparation manual MAN-10185-01-1 (NanoString).

In summary, the 5 µm FFPE sections were placed onto the 15 mm x 22 mm scannable area of a Superfrost Plus Micro Slide and dried in an oven at 37 °C for 12:00:00 , seal with desiccant and dried for a further 12:00:00 at Room temperature before storage at 4 °C .
The slides were baked at 65 °C for 02:00:00 and then equilibrated to Room temperature for 00:03:00 - 00:05:00 , and then washed twice in xylene (or CitriSolv) for 5 minutes each at Room temperature .
Washed in xylene (or CitriSolv) for 00:05:00 at Room temperature (1/2).
Washed in xylene (or CitriSolv) for 00:05:00 at Room temperature (2/2).
The slides were washed twice in 100% ethanol for 10 minutes each, twice in 95% ethanol for 5 minutes each, and once in 70% ethanol for 5 minutes, followed by two washes in 1X PBS for 5 minutes each.
The slides were washed in 100% ethanol for 00:10:00 (1/2).
The slides were washed in 100% ethanol for 00:10:00 (2/2).
The slides were washed in 95% ethanol for 00:05:00 (1/2).
The slides were washed in 95% ethanol for 00:05:00 (2/2).
The slides were washed in 70% ethanol for 00:05:00 (1/1).
Followed by a wash in 1X PBS for 00:05:00 (1/2).
Followed by a wash in 1X PBS for 00:05:00 (2/2).

The slides were immersed in pre-heated 1X Target Retrieval solution and heated in a pressure cooker at 100 °C with the valve closed for 00:15:00 , then allowed to equilibrate to Room temperature in the same solution.
Afterward, the slides were washed three times in 1X PBS for 5 minutes each, and incubation frames were attached.
The slides were washed in 1X PBS for 00:05:00 (1/3).
The slides were washed in 1X PBS for 00:05:00 (2/3).
The slides were washed in 1X PBS for 00:05:00 (3/3).
Tissue sections were then covered with Buffer W and incubated at Room temperature for 01:00:00 in the dark.
The primary antibody mix was incubated at 4 °C for 17:00:00 , followed by three washes with 1X TBS-T buffer for 10 minutes each and once with 1X PBS for 2 minutes.
Followed by a wash with 1X TBS-T buffer for 00:10:00 (1/3).
Followed by a wash with 1X TBS-T buffer for 00:10:00 (2/3).
Followed by a wash with 1X TBS-T buffer for 00:10:00 (3/3).
Followed by a wash with 1X PBS for 00:02:00 .
Fiducials, prepared at the recommended concentration of 0.00005%, applied to the slides at Room temperature for 00:05:00 , protected from light.
Slides were then washed once with 1X PBS for 00:05:00 and fixed with 4% PFA for 00:15:00 .
Followed by three washes in 1X PBS for 5 minutes each.
Followed by a wash in 1X PBS for 00:05:00 each (1/3).
Followed by a wash in 1X PBS for 00:05:00 each (2/3).
Followed by a wash in 1X PBS for 00:05:00 each (3/3).

Sections were stained with nuclei stain for 00:10:00 , washed twice with 1X PBS for 5 minutes, and incubated with 100 millimolar (mM) NHS acetate for 00:05:00 , before a final wash in 1X PBS for 00:05:00 .
Followed by a wash with 1X PBS for 00:05:00 (1/2).

Followed by a wash with 1X PBS for 00:05:00 (2/2). The selected pre-bleaching profile was configuration C, and the cell segmentation profile was configuration A. These procedures were uniformly followed at St. Jude Children's Research Hospital, Weill Cornell Medical Center, and the University of Adelaide, ensuring consistency in the fields of view (FOVs) across all three locations. Breast cancer, normal appendix, and normal ileum samples processed as part of the NanoString’s Technology Access Program followed the methods described in Reference https://doi.org/10.1038/s41587-022-01483-z .

The NanoString 64-plex human protein antibody panel was combined with CD298/B2M and PanCK/CD45 segmentation markers in Buffer W. Samples from the appendix, ileum, and pancreas were also incubated with the CD68 segmentation marker.
